best_for: Next.js App Router projects wanting official-feel i18n: type-safe messages, server component support, built-in per-locale routing
tradeoffs: Mature and well-documented for Next.js; message files can grow large without a TMS

best_for: Compile-time-checked, tree-shaken translations (Inlang ecosystem) with zero runtime overhead across frameworks
avoid_when: Teams already committed to a framework-native i18n convention
tradeoffs: Smallest bundle impact of the mainstream options; younger ecosystem than next-intl or react-i18next

best_for: Framework-agnostic React (CRA, Vite, React Native) or non-Next stacks with the widest plugin ecosystem
avoid_when: Next.js App Router where next-intl's server-component support fits better
tradeoffs: Huge ecosystem and plugin support; more configuration surface than newer compile-time tools

best_for: Teams standardizing on the ICU MessageFormat spec directly across React and vanilla JS with strict pluralization/gender rules
avoid_when: Wanting the simplest possible setup since ICU syntax has a learning curve
tradeoffs: Spec-correct ICU handling for plurals and gender; more verbose message authoring than simpler key-value tools

option: Static UI strings in message files
best_for: Interface chrome such as buttons, labels, nav, and error messages kept as JSON/YAML per locale and reviewed via PR
avoid_when: Content that changes independently of a deploy
tradeoffs: Type-safe and deploy-atomic; every UI string change requires a code deploy

best_for: CMS-driven or user-facing dynamic content that non-developers edit, with locale as a column or table rather than a message file
avoid_when: Interface chrome where a message file is simpler
tradeoffs: Content editors work independently of deploys; needs a defined fallback-locale query pattern and its own schema discipline

best_for: Teams with three or more locales and non-developer translators who need translation memory and context screenshots
avoid_when: A single extra locale maintained directly by the dev team
tradeoffs: Removes translators from the git workflow and reduces mistranslation; adds a vendor and a sync step

best_for: Fast initial coverage for a new locale before a native-speaker reviewer approves the draft
avoid_when: Legal medical or brand-critical copy shipped without review
tradeoffs: Cuts initial translation time dramatically; unreviewed MT output damages trust faster than missing translations do

best_for: Small locale counts where team members already speak the target languages or copy is highly technical or brand-sensitive
avoid_when: Scaling past a handful of locales
tradeoffs: Full control and zero vendor cost; throughput is capped by team bandwidth not tooling

best_for: All locale-aware formatting including Intl.DateTimeFormat, Intl.NumberFormat, Intl.ListFormat, and Intl.PluralRules with zero dependencies
avoid_when: Needing a format the Intl API genuinely does not cover which is rare
tradeoffs: Ships in every modern runtime with no bundle cost; more verbose than a wrapper library
